Blocked Drains Swindon provides professional drain repair services across Swindon and the surrounding areas, addressing everything from minor pipe damage to complete drainage system failures. Over time, drainage pipes can deteriorate due to age, ground movement, tree root intrusion, or general wear and tear. When left unaddressed, damaged drains can lead to persistent blockages, foul odours, subsidence, and even structural damage to your property.

Our drain repair services encompass a wide range of solutions designed to restore your drainage system to full working order. We begin every job with a thorough diagnostic assessment, typically using CCTV drain survey technology to identify the exact location and nature of the damage. For pipes that have suffered cracks, fractures, or joint displacement but remain structurally intact, we often recommend pipe relining as a no-dig alternative to traditional excavation. This modern technique involves inserting a resin-impregnated liner into the existing pipe, which is then cured in place to create a new pipe within the old one. Pipe relining is faster, less disruptive, and often more economical than traditional dig-and-replace methods.

Where damage is more extensive, such as collapsed pipes, severe root intrusion, or significant misalignment, drain excavation may be necessary. Our experienced team is fully equipped to carry out careful excavation work, removing and replacing damaged pipe sections with high-quality materials that will stand the test of time. We always aim to minimise disruption to your property and restore the ground surface to its original condition once work is complete.

subService:drain-repairs:pipe-relining:
Blocked Drains Swindon offers professional pipe relining services across Swindon and the surrounding areas, providing a modern, no-dig solution for repairing damaged drains. Pipe relining, also known as cured-in-place pipe lining, is a trenchless technology that allows us to repair cracked, fractured, or leaking pipes from the inside, without the need for disruptive excavation work.

The pipe relining process involves inserting a flexible liner coated with a specialist resin into the damaged pipe. Once in position, the liner is inflated to press against the walls of the existing pipe, and the resin is cured using heat or UV light to form a hard, seamless new pipe within the old one. The result is a structurally sound, watertight repair that can extend the life of your drainage system by decades.

Pipe relining is suitable for a wide range of drainage problems, including cracks and fractures in the pipe wall, displaced or leaking joints, minor root intrusion damage, and localised corrosion. It is particularly well-suited to situations where the existing pipe is still largely intact but has developed faults that compromise its function. The technique can be used on pipes of various materials, including clay, cast iron, concrete, and plastic.

One of the key advantages of pipe relining is that it avoids the need for extensive digging. Traditional drain repairs often require excavation of gardens, driveways, patios, or even interior floors to access the damaged pipe. Pipe relining eliminates this disruption, as the work is carried out through existing access points such as manholes or inspection chambers. This makes it a faster, cleaner, and often more cost-effective alternative to traditional methods.

At Blocked Drains Swindon, our engineers are fully trained and experienced in the latest pipe relining techniques. We begin every relining job with a detailed CCTV drain survey to assess the condition of the pipe and confirm that relining is the appropriate solution. Once the work is complete, we carry out a further survey to verify the quality of the repair and ensure that your drainage system is functioning correctly.

If you are dealing with recurring drainage problems and would like to explore pipe relining as a repair option, contact Blocked Drains Swindon today at 01793487489. Our team will be happy to assess your situation and advise on the best solution for your property.
